rct_screen_evidence: "Eligible patients were centrally randomly assigned in the Alea Clinical Portal), in a 1:1:1:1 ratio, to SOC, SOC plus radiotherapy, SOC plus abiraterone (abiraterone plus prednisone), or SOC plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone. This randomisation process was performed via the Tenalea autonomous software... Randomisation was done using a minimisation algorithm, stratified by study site, ECOG performance status... No masking was performed in this study."
intervention: Abiraterone (1000 mg orally once daily) plus prednisone (5 mg orally twice daily) added to standard of care
comparator: Standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² every 3 weeks)

1.1.quote: "Randomisation was done using a minimisation algorithm, stratified by study site, ECOG performance status..., with 80% probability to minimise imbalance in the number of patients assigned to each treatment group." (Randomisation_section)
1.1.justification: The description of a computer‑based minimisation algorithm constitutes a recognized random method.

1.2.quote: "Eligible patients were centrally randomly assigned in the Alea Clinical Portal..., performed via the Tenalea autonomous software, solely accessed by the trial data manager..." (Randomisation_section)
1.2.justification: Centralised, software‑driven allocation performed by an independent data manager ensures concealment until enrolment.

1.3.quote: "Table 1: Baseline characteristics in the intention-to-treat population" (Baseline_characteristics) – the presented numbers show comparable group sizes and balanced baseline variables.
1.3.justification: Baseline characteristics are broadly similar across arms, indicating no evidence of a problem with randomisation.

2.1.quote: "We conducted an open-label, randomised, activecontrolled, phase 3 study…" (Methods)
2.1.justification: The trial is explicitly described as open‑label, indicating participants knew their assigned treatment.

2.2.quote: "We conducted an open-label, randomised, activecontrolled, phase 3 study…" (Methods)
2.2.justification: Because the study is open‑label, healthcare providers and trial personnel delivering the interventions were also aware of group allocation.

3.1.justification: Less than 5% of randomized participants lacked outcome data, meeting the >95% threshold for “nearly all”.

4.1.justification: Standard RECIST imaging is a validated, objective method.

4.2.quote: "open‑label, randomised" (blinding_section) – no indication of different imaging schedules.
4.2.justification: Both arms used the same imaging protocol.

4.3.justification: Assessors were not blinded to treatment allocation.

4.4.justification: Interpretation of imaging can involve judgment, so knowledge could influence assessment.

4.5.justification: Standardised RECIST criteria limit subjective influence despite open‑label design.

5.1.quote: "The coprimary endpoints were radiographic progression-free survival and overall survival." (Methods section)
5.1.justification: The outcome (progression-free survival) was pre‑specified as a co‑primary endpoint in the trial protocol.

5.2.quote: "Adjusted Cox regression modelling revealed no interaction between abiraterone and radiotherapy, enabling the pooled analysis of abiraterone efficacy." (Results section)
5.2.justification: Only a single, pre‑specified definition of radiographic progression‑free survival was used; no alternative measurements were presented.

5.3.quote: "All efficacy analyses were conducted in the intention‑to‑treat population... The Cox proportional hazards model adjusted for radiotherapy and stratification factors provided significances and an estimate of the abiraterone effect." (Statistical analysis plan)
5.3.justification: The analysis follows the pre‑specified statistical analysis plan with no evidence of post‑hoc selective analytic choices.

2.3.justification: No trial-context-driven crossovers or contamination are reported.

2.6.quote: "All efficacy analyses were conducted in the intention-to-treat population, defined as all patients who were randomly assigned to a treatment group." (Statistical analysis)
2.6.justification: The study explicitly states that the primary effect was estimated using an ITT analysis, which is appropriate for assessing the effect of assignment.

domain_rationales.D2: Part1=Low (Part1 Low condition met); Part2=Low (2.6=Y/PY (appropriate ITT analysis) -> Part2=Low)
